codeReview.summary: Implementation successfully fulfills all expected behaviors for restart reconciliation and recovery. The code is well-structured, thoroughly tested, and correctly integrated into the startup flow. All 191 tests pass, TypeScript validates cleanly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly added comprehensive documentation to .factory/library/restart-reconciliation.md following the established pattern. The documentation covers worktree reconciliation, missing worktree handling, session recovery, and UI selection repair.

Worker followed the architectural separation: reconciliation logic in RestartReconciliation.ts (service layer), integration in App.tsx (presentation layer), and persistence updates via existing repositories. No business logic leaked into components.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/worktree/RestartReconciliation.ts handles pure reconciliation; App.tsx:97-103 calls it before loading worktrees into UI

sharedStateObservations.observation: Excellent test coverage with 8 new test cases covering: interrupted session recovery for all 4 active states (queued/starting/running/cancelling), missing worktree detection, valid worktree inclusion, mixed scenarios, and end-to-end semantics (no recreation, subsequent restarts).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/worktree/RestartReconciliation.test.ts:392 lines, 8 test cases with actual git worktree operations

summary: Feature implementation is complete and correct. The RestartReconciliation service properly compares persisted worktrees against git worktree state, marks missing worktrees as cleanup_failed without recreation, converts interrupted sessions to recovered non-active state, and integrates correctly with App.tsx startup flow. All validation criteria (VAL-LIFECYCLE-004, VAL-LIFECYCLE-006, VAL-CROSS-003, VAL-CROSS-004) are satisfied. 191 tests pass, TypeScript validates cleanly.
